title: Living in Boulder Vlog 2 - 20 - 2017: American Tribes in Background

enrichment.summary: Kyle films a late-night vlog wearing a Joker hat, briefly explains a fictional story he's writing about characters called 'seers' and 'jokers' in a Boulder setting, then discusses a background map showing American tribal territories and how cities like Santa Fe and Rome served as tribal meeting/trading points. He recaps his day: car repairs (a faulty interior light), and working on a 313-page audit/history book project for America.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Initially disliked it but thinks it looks cool on video

stances.topic: Capital cities as tribal meeting points
stances.position: Believes major cities historically functioned as trading posts where different tribes converged, using Santa Fe and Rome as examples
Pragmatic — will fix the brakes and sell the car as-is rather than investing more in it

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-log. It's exactly midnight at 1221, so for the 20th.
Somebody wanted me to wear my Joker hat.
So I didn't like this hat, but I think it actually looks pretty cool on the video.
This is one of my characters in the Kingly Court of Boulder Rome.
He's a little bit at odds with me, with my lineage, for a little bit because he sees us.
It's like competition. We're officially the Seers, and they're officially, you know, like the Jokers,
And the singers and the dancers, and so we try to, we like to sing and dance too,
So we have to come up with a compromise.
But anyways, I haven't written that story yet.
Oh shit, the background, okay.
Yeah, so this background is just like, oh yeah, American tribes.
And when you zoom in, I went into Santa Fe.
You can see how Santa Fe is where three different tribes meet.
So I think like big capital cities, that's where different tribes meet.
It's like a trading post probably, back in tribal times.
So Rome was built right on the border too, the Etruscans.
And then I zoomed up and I was looking around at all the tribes so you can get an idea of how big the tribes are,
Like how big the territory is.
Because, you know, they couldn't do that in tribal times.
But now we can.
Yeah, some tribes I guess could, if they go to the top of a mountain.
Maybe at the top of the Heisman, you can barely see the edges, like the small tribes.
But they're all different kinds of sizes.
In Herodotus, he talks about a tribe of like Chinese people who's just like a group of people.
But they're like, you know, holy people.
So, you know, people let them be their own group.
Anyway, so what did I do today?
I got up early today, even though I had insomnia all night.
I had insomnia, but I got a rest and I actually got up at 8th.
And I was working for a couple hours and I went to try to do my car, do stuff for my car.
So, yeah, I wanted to get the light fixed on the inside.
So I drove that down there.
But then I thought maybe it was, she thought, well, is it a light bulb?
So, yeah, I thought it'd maybe take a light bulb.
So I drove a whole town, like the third place I went to, I found a light bulb.
But it didn't work, so I drove it back.
So, you know, what happened was back in July, I paid them $80 to fix it, the light inside the van.
And then, you know, now that I want to sell it again.
Because it broke right after I went to sell it.
And so now I decided to sell it and it breaks again.
So hopefully they can sell it, they can fix it again.
And then I can sell it.
I'm not going to try to fix the radio.
I'll just sell it as it is.
I think it's clean enough, too.
But tomorrow I have to take in the vacuum to my mom's vacuum and get it fixed.
So today, yeah, then I went into, came home and worked on my free audio history books for America.
That was a total disaster area.
I had no idea.
So, yeah, I pretty much made it look right today.
And it's 313 pages worth of text.
So it's like writing a book.
But I'll explain all that later.
But, yeah, I'll just fix the links tomorrow.
And then I do UK.
And then that section's done.
It's, yeah, super exciting.
So, yeah, I'll find out about my car tomorrow.
And I guess I'll be peacing out right now because the video's over.
Ciao.
